arts.ttl: Newcastle do not have buy-back clause for highly-rated duo they sold in 2024
arts.sum: Newcastle United sold Elliot Anderson and Yankuba Minteh to comply with financial regulations at the start of the season. Both players have been very impressive for their respective clubs, and there have been talks on whether Newcastle could look to bring them back to the club in future. According to a report from Daily Mail, […] The post Newcastle do not have buy-back clause for highly-rated duo they sold in 2024 appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United owners' £15m move explained that's 'good for PSR' after Companies House update
arts.sum: One such capital injection in 2022 was worth £70.4m following investment in the squad, a training ground upgrade, improvements to St James' Park and the hiring of a number of senior executives to build out the commercial operations of the club. CEO Darren Eales said at the time that the 'additional investment further enables us to continue implementing the business plan' and the club confirmed a year ago that the ownership group had since 'injected additional capital to improve the financial position of the business'.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United diehards reassured after West Ham's 'incredibly strange' Crown Prince confusion
arts.sum: It was just a few weeks ago that Newcastle United were namechecked 20 times inside the chamber after fellow peer Lord Moynihan asked Baroness Twycross, the parliamentary under-secretary of state at the department for culture, media and sport, if Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman would be subject to an ownership test. A government minister gave an 'incredibly strange' response after being asked if Saudi Arabia's Crown Prince would have to undergo the independent football regulator's owners' and directors' test.

arts.ttl: Barcelona want Newcastle United star in January
arts.sum: The brass at La Liga giants Barcelona have set their sights on the signature of Newcastle United star Alexander Isak for the January transfer window. That’s according to Mundo Deportivo,…

arts.ttl: The one club who could afford Alexander Isak as Alan Shearer issues Newcastle United transfer claim
arts.sum: Only one Premier League side could possibly afford the amout of money it would take to prise Alexander Isak away from Newcastle United, according to Alan Shearer. "Other than possibly Chelsea, I genuinely don’t think that anybody could afford to buy Alexander Isak," Shearer told Betfair.

arts.ttl: Martin Dubravka handed Newcastle United ultimatum as huge decision looms
arts.sum: But as Chronicle Live reported on Friday, Howe said even if Dubravka stayed and played a couple more times this season that would be deemed the "right decision" from a football point of view - especially if he helps Newcastle book their place in the Carabao Cup final. But Newcastle have so far delayed his exit from St James' Park due to Nick Pope's knee injury.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta Arsenal transfer admission is just what Newcastle United want to hear
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta beileves it will be very difficult for Arsenal to sign players who can affect their first team in the January transfer window. "First of all, someone that can impact the team, bringing in bodies doesn't help us all," Arteta said when asked about transfers.

arts.ttl: Chelsea make 'we knew Newcastle United would' claim and address Marc Guehi transfer talk
arts.sum: Chelsea, in contrast, have only won one league game in that time and the fourth-placed Blues are now just a point ahead of Newcastle. However, Newcastle have since won five league games on the bounce; scored 14 goals; conceded just once; and defeated, among others, Aston Villa, Spurs and Manchester United in the top-flight.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United sent £30m transfer reminder with Premier League deal 'close'
arts.sum: Eddie Howe continues to remind everyone that this month will consist of outgoings before incomings as the Magpies keep a close eye on their coffers amid ongoing PSR concerns. It promises to be a quieter month than many would like on Tyneside as Newcastle United ponder how to navigate a winter window with limited funds at their disposal.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United's smart PSR move sees Magpies land seven-figure windall
arts.sum: The shirt, famously worn by David Ginola, Rob Lee, Alan Shearer and Les Ferdinand, was launched by the Magpies at the club shop this week with the help of the former skipper Lee and flying winger Keith Gillespie. We had some great kits but this one with the grandad collar and wider stripes was just iconic with Newcastle.
